anathema: maneuver around an enemy when tumbling through their space is a viable option, use safety measures to attempt acrobatic stunts
edict: leap without being sure you’ll land unharmed, test your reflexes by maneuvering through the spaces of significantly more powerful enemies
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Mythic Calling " pfs="">[Acrobat's Calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=1)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Calling" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=834" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 79

You feel a need to dance amid dangerous heights and soar across empty divides with nothing but your skill and reflexes to protect you. When you [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369), [Maneuver in Flight](/Actions.aspx?ID=2371), [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=2370), or attempt a [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378), you can spend a Mythic Point to attempt the check at mythic proficiency. The first time each day that you critically succeed on an [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) check while Balancing, Maneuvering in Flight, or Tumbling Through during combat, you regain a Mythic Point.

---

**Edicts** leap without being sure you’ll land unharmed, test your reflexes by maneuvering through the spaces of significantly more powerful enemies

**Anathema** maneuver around an enemy when tumbling through their space is a viable option, use safety measures to attempt acrobatic stunts
name: Acrobat's Calling

</traits>

<additional-info>

anathema: deny a call for an encore performance, refuse a request to perform in front of an audience, sabotage another performer
edict: master new performances, seek opportunities to perform in front of new audiences, support other performers
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Mythic Calling " pfs="">[Thespian's Calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=10)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Calling" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=834" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 80

All the world is a stage, and you’re no mere player, but a playwright whose story may change the course of history. When you use [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) to [Perform](/Actions.aspx?ID=2401) or [Earn Income](/Rules.aspx?ID=2635), you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) to attempt the check at mythic proficiency. The first time each day that you critically succeed on a check to Perform, you regain a Mythic Point.

---

**Edicts** master new performances, seek opportunities to perform in front of new audiences, support other performers

**Anathema** deny a call for an encore performance, refuse a request to perform in front of an audience, sabotage another performer
name: Thespian's Calling

anathema: leave unattended treasure where you found it, pass up opportunities for profit that challenge your skills
edict: seek opportunities to overcome evermore dangerous and complex locks and mechanical defenses, steal things from dangerous creatures or places
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Mythic Calling " pfs="">[Thief's Calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=11)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Calling" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=834" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 81

Whether guildmaster or cutpurse, you covet something, or many things, that don’t belong to you, and you’re driven to ever greater heights of larceny. When you attempt to [Pick a Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=2412) or [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2410) an item, you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) to attempt the check at mythic proficiency. The first time each day that you critically succeed on a check to Pick a Lock or Steal from a [hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=80) creature, you regain a Mythic Point.

---

**Edicts** seek opportunities to overcome evermore dangerous and complex locks and mechanical defenses, steal things from dangerous creatures or places

**Anathema** leave unattended treasure where you found it, pass up opportunities for profit that challenge your skills
name: Thief's Calling

anathema: knowingly aid in any plot to end the world, mock or deride those who predict the end of the world
edict: help to prevent (or at least forestall) doomsday by keeping allies (and their gear) in good health (or repair), console those who are despondent
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Mythic Calling " pfs="">[Doomsayer's Calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=13)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Calling" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=834" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

**Source** [Revenge of the Runelords Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=282) pg. 17

As long as people have dwelt in the world, there have been those who foresaw, feared, or worked toward that world’s end. You know all things must end and have long felt that at some point in your life, you would stand on the precipice of Golarion’s final fate—not to usher it along, but to deny the agents of this doomsday the satisfaction of seeing your world end. Until then, you’ll do your best to keep the world and your allies in shape to face these ultimate threats. When you attempt a check to [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=2384), [Administer First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2396), or [Treat Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=2399) (but not when you use [Battle Medicine](/Feats.aspx?ID=5125)), you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323) to attempt the check at [mythic proficiency](/Rules.aspx?ID=3322). The first time each day that you critically succeed on a check to Repair or Administer First Aid, you regain a Mythic Point.

---

**Edicts** help to prevent (or at least forestall) doomsday by keeping allies (and their gear) in good health (or repair), console those who are despondent

**Anathema** knowingly aid in any plot to end the world, mock or deride those who predict the end of the world
name: Doomsayer's Calling
primary_source: Revenge of the Runelords Player's Guide
primary_source_raw: Revenge of the Runelords Player's Guide pg. 17

anathema: deny a creature (including yourself) the chance to sleep long enough to dream, deride those who have ambitions that they’re unlikely to ever achieve
edict: share your dreams with your friends and take interest if they share their dreams with you, take inspiration from your dreams when you create
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Mythic Calling " pfs="">[Dreamer's Calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=14)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Calling" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=834" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

**Source** [Revenge of the Runelords Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=282) pg. 17

Your dreams have always felt more real than reality itself, and upon learning of the Dimension of Dreams you weren’t surprised—indeed, you’ve often found yourself entering these Dreamlands when you sleep. You’ve long felt that something in the Dreamlands has been calling to you, and that some great rescue or revelation awaits in a dream you’ve yet to experience. In the meantime, your dreams remain a potent source of inspiration and power, and the ability to draw from them to affect the waking world is something you’ve always been able to do. When you attempt an [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43), [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46), or [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367), or when you attempt a check to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) any work of art, you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323) to attempt the check at [mythic proficiency](/Rules.aspx?ID=3322). The first time each day that you critically succeed at a check to Craft or at a Recall Knowledge check about a subject associated with dreaming or the Dreamlands, you regain a Mythic Point.

---

**Edicts** share your dreams with your friends and take interest if they share their dreams with you, take inspiration from your dreams when you create

**Anathema** deny a creature (including yourself) the chance to sleep long enough to dream, deride those who have ambitions that they’re unlikely to ever achieve
name: Dreamer's Calling
primary_source: Revenge of the Runelords Player's Guide
primary_source_raw: Revenge of the Runelords Player's Guide pg. 17

anathema: willingly destroy ancient texts or other forms of written knowledge without first preserving that lore in some way, treat another’s convictions or beliefs as fantasy or delusion
edict: seek out rare magic spells and items from ancient Thassilon, learn knowledge about Thassilon that has been forgotten
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Mythic Calling " pfs="">[Runelord's Calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=15)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Calling" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=834" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

**Source** [Revenge of the Runelords Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=282) pg. 18

Even before Karzoug rose, you felt something tugging to you from beyond. Strange memories, sudden insights, vivid dreams, or wild hunches that lead to discoveries or unexpected successes have been the norm for you. Once knowledge of the legacies of Thassilon began to spread anew into the modern world after Karzoug rose from slumber, you’ve increasingly come to realize these visions and inspirations come to you from the past—maybe even one of your past lives—and that something from the time before Earthfall in ancient Thassilon is linked to your soul. When you attempt a check to [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=2365), [Learn a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2366), or cast a ritual, you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323) to attempt the check at [mythic proficiency](/Rules.aspx?ID=3322). The first time each day that you critically succeed at an [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) check to Identify Magic, you regain a Mythic Point.

---

**Edicts** seek out rare magic spells and items from ancient Thassilon, learn knowledge about Thassilon that has been forgotten

**Anathema** willingly destroy ancient texts or other forms of written knowledge without first preserving that lore in some way, treat another’s convictions or beliefs as fantasy or delusion
name: Runelord's Calling
primary_source: Revenge of the Runelords Player's Guide
primary_source_raw: Revenge of the Runelords Player's Guide pg. 18

anathema: allow those in the Saga Lands who need aid to suffer if there’s something you can do to help them, regard your own saga as more important than anyone else’s, since your tale has yet to be fully told—and not even you know its ending
edict: do what you can to protect the people of the Saga Lands, preserve legends and myths in writing or other forms of art for future generations to enjoy and learn from
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Mythic Calling " pfs="">[Saga's Calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=16)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Calling" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=834" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

**Source** [Revenge of the Runelords Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=282) pg. 18

The Saga Lands do not come by that appellation lightly, for the storied legacies of this region predate Earthfall and have roots in worlds far beyond Golarion. Whether you were born in these lands or drawn there, those storied legacies have resonated with you on a supernatural level. It seems like each time you learn of some new legend or tale of this land passed down through the generations, there’s something in it that directly applies to your life. You’ve taken to relying upon these legends—be they ancient or modern—as guideposts for your own life’s course, knowing full well that they’re calling to you and that you’re destined to be the focus of one yourself.

When you attempt a [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check or a check to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=2391), you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323) to attempt the check at [mythic proficiency](/Rules.aspx?ID=3322). The first time each day that you critically succeed at a check to Gather Information, you regain a Mythic Point.

---

**Edicts** do what you can to protect the people of the Saga Lands, preserve legends and myths in writing or other forms of art for future generations to enjoy and learn from

**Anathema** allow those in the Saga Lands who need aid to suffer if there’s something you can do to help them, regard your own saga as more important than anyone else’s, since your tale has yet to be fully told—and not even you know its ending
name: Saga's Calling
primary_source: Revenge of the Runelords Player's Guide
primary_source_raw: Revenge of the Runelords Player's Guide pg. 18

anathema: destroy the creations of other artisans, refuse an earnest request to repair something that has been [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60), use a weapon or item crafted by someone else, except for the purpose of learning its function so you can understand how to create it yourself
edict: be the first to test an item or weapon crafted with your own hands, seek out or innovate formulas for evermore complex creations
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Mythic Calling " pfs="">[Artisan's Calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=2)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Calling" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=834" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 79

You feel an urge that echoes throughout your being to create, and one day you know you shall craft an achievement surpassing anything the world has ever seen. When you [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) or [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=2384) an object (including [vehicles](/Rules.aspx?ID=3116) and [structures](/Rules.aspx?ID=2944)), you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) to attempt the check at mythic proficiency. The first time each day that you critically succeed on a check to Repair an object, you regain a Mythic Point.

---

**Edicts** be the first to test an item or weapon crafted with your own hands, seek out or innovate formulas for evermore complex creations

**Anathema** destroy the creations of other artisans, refuse an earnest request to repair something that has been [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60), use a weapon or item crafted by someone else, except for the purpose of learning its function so you can understand how to create it yourself
name: Artisan's Calling

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Calling" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=834" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<additional-info>
**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 79
</additional-info>

---

<summary>
You feel an urge that echoes throughout your being to create, and one day you know you shall craft an achievement surpassing anything the world has …
</summary>
summary: You feel an urge that echoes throughout your being to create, and one day you know you shall craft an achievement surpassing anything the world has …
summary_markdown: You feel an urge that echoes throughout your being to create, and one day you know you shall craft an achievement surpassing anything the world has …
text: Artisan's Calling Source War of Immortals pg. 79 You feel an urge that echoes throughout your being to create, and one day you know you shall craft an achievement surpassing anything the world has ever seen. When you Craft or Repair an object (including vehicles and structures), you can spend a Mythic Point to attempt the check at mythic proficiency. The first time each day that you critically succeed on a check to Repair an object, you regain a Mythic Point. --- Edicts be the first to test an item or weapon crafted with your own hands, seek out or innovate formulas for evermore complex creations Anathema destroy the creations of other artisans, refuse an earnest request to repair something that has been broken, use a weapon or item crafted by someone else, except for the purpose of learning its function so you can understand how to create it yourself

anathema: destroy books, scrolls, or other instruments used to record information; refuse an opportunity to share your knowledge with an earnest knowledge-seeker
edict: educate others, record new knowledge so that others can share in your discoveries
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Mythic Calling " pfs="">[Bookkeeper's Calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=3)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Calling" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=834" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 79

Your passion and destiny are both tied to the preservation of knowledge and a deep-seated love of instruments designed to preserve and propagate knowledge, such as books and scrolls. When you attempt a check to [Recall Knowledge](/Rules.aspx?ID=2638) or [Earn Income](/Rules.aspx?ID=2635) using a [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) skill you’re trained in, you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) to attempt the check at mythic proficiency. The first time each day that you critically succeed on a check to Recall Knowledge while researching in a library or similar facility, you regain a Mythic Point.

---

**Edicts** educate others, record new knowledge so that others can share in your discoveries

**Anathema** destroy books, scrolls, or other instruments used to record information; refuse an opportunity to share your knowledge with an earnest knowledge-seeker
name: Bookkeeper's Calling

anathema: deny care to the disadvantaged, refuse to heal the wounds of an enemy who has surrendered
edict: give aid to others, heal those who are wounded regardless of where they come from
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Mythic Calling " pfs="">[Caretaker's Calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=4)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Calling" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=834" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 80

You have a deep-seated drive or divine directive to care for others, and your power might one day grow to allow you to heal an unimaginable wound. When you [Treat Disease](/Actions.aspx?ID=2397), [Treat Poison](/Actions.aspx?ID=2398), [Treat Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=2399), or use [Battle Medicine](/Feats.aspx?ID=5125), you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) to attempt the check at mythic proficiency. The first time each day that you critically succeed on a check to Treat Wounds, you regain a Mythic Point.

---

**Edicts** give aid to others, heal those who are wounded regardless of where they come from

**Anathema** deny care to the disadvantaged, refuse to heal the wounds of an enemy who has surrendered
name: Caretaker's Calling

anathema: interrupt someone telling a story, resort to violent action without trying to defuse the situation with your words
edict: tell stories to audiences who have never heard them before, use words rather than weapons to resolve conflicts
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Mythic Calling " pfs="">[Demagogue's Calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=5)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Calling" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=834" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 80

Words flow freely from a font within your soul. When you attempt a check to [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=2394) or [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392), you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) to attempt the check at mythic proficiency. The first time each day that you critically succeed on a check to Coerce a creature who isn’t your ally, Create a Diversion using your words, or Make an Impression on an [indifferent](/Conditions.aspx?ID=82) or [unfriendly](/Conditions.aspx?ID=97) audience, you regain a Mythic Point.

---

**Edicts** tell stories to audiences who have never heard them before, use words rather than weapons to resolve conflicts

**Anathema** interrupt someone telling a story, resort to violent action without trying to defuse the situation with your words
name: Demagogue's Calling

anathema: retreat from a battle while your allies or innocent bystanders are in danger without taking every reasonable effort to rescue them first
edict: place yourself in harm’s way to protect your allies or innocent creatures under threat
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Mythic Calling " pfs="">[Guardian's Calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=6)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Calling" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=834" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 80

Your instinctive urge is always to protect others, pushing back aggressors and doing everything in your power to prevent them from harming your allies and charges. When you attempt to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383), [Reposition](/Actions.aspx?ID=2379), or [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) an opponent, you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) to make the check at mythic proficiency. You can attempt these maneuvers against a creature of any size when performing them at mythic proficiency. The first time each day that you critically succeed on a check to Disarm, Reposition, or Shove an opponent, you regain a Mythic Point.

---

**Edicts** place yourself in harm’s way to protect your allies or innocent creatures under threat

**Anathema** retreat from a battle while your allies or innocent bystanders are in danger without taking every reasonable effort to rescue them first
name: Guardian's Calling

anathema: initiate combat against an animal or beast encountered in its natural environment, kill an animal or beast when sparing it is a viable option
edict: care for dangerous animals or beasts in need, respect the habitats of wild creatures
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Mythic Calling " pfs="">[Handler's Calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=7)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Calling" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=834" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 80

You have an instinctive urge to care for wild creatures. When you attempt a check to [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2400), you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) to attempt the check at mythic proficiency. The first time each day that you critically succeed on a check to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) on a wild animal or Command an Animal who isn’t your pet or companion, you regain a Mythic Point.

---

**Edicts** care for dangerous animals or beasts in need, respect the habitats of wild creatures

**Anathema** initiate combat against an animal or beast encountered in its natural environment, kill an animal or beast when sparing it is a viable option
name: Handler's Calling

anathema: leave an animal or beast you’ve wounded free to cause future harm, refuse a request to hunt a dangerous animal or beast
edict: be first in a group to scout an unknown and potentially dangerous area, seek out creatures that are causing imbalances in the natural world and correct the imbalance

<title level="1" right="Mythic Calling " pfs="">[Hunter's Calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=8)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Calling" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=834" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 80

You’re the hunter, and somewhere, a legendary predator awaits as your ultimate challenge. When you attempt a check to [Seek](/Rules.aspx?ID=2554) or [Track](/Rules.aspx?ID=2645), you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) to attempt the check at mythic proficiency. The first time each day that you critically succeed on a check to Seek or Track a [hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=80) or [unfriendly](/Conditions.aspx?ID=97) creature, you regain a Mythic Point.

---

**Edicts** be first in a group to scout an unknown and potentially dangerous area, seek out creatures that are causing imbalances in the natural world and correct the imbalance

**Anathema** leave an animal or beast you’ve wounded free to cause future harm, refuse a request to hunt a dangerous animal or beast
name: Hunter's Calling

anathema: destroy books or other repositories of knowledge, lie to hide knowledge from the world
edict: preserve your knowledge for future generations, seek out lost knowledge in dangerous or forgotten places
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Mythic Calling " pfs="">[Sage's Calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=9)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Calling" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=834" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 80

You’re already an archive of knowledge, but you’re driven to learn more with an unquenchable passion that might never be sated. When you attempt to [Recall Knowledge](/Rules.aspx?ID=2638), you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) to attempt the check at mythic proficiency. The first time each day that you critically succeed on a Recall Knowledge check against an enemy creature, you regain a Mythic Point.

---

**Edicts** preserve your knowledge for future generations, seek out lost knowledge in dangerous or forgotten places

**Anathema** destroy books or other repositories of knowledge, lie to hide knowledge from the world
name: Sage's Calling
